items.q: What is the worst-case complexity of bubble sort?
items.misconception: O(n) because it just passes through the list.
items.why: Students count one pass and forget that bubble sort does up to n passes, each comparing up to n elements. The nested loop structure gives n times n = n squared.

items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.q: What is the main advantage of asymmetric encryption?
items.correct: Secure key exchange without sharing a secret in advance.
items.misconception: It is faster than symmetric encryption.
items.why: Students think asymmetric is just 'better'. It's actually SLOWER than symmetric. Its advantage is solving key distribution - the public key can be shared openly without compromising security.
Correct answer: TCP. “UDP” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What does HTTPS provide that HTTP does not?
items.why: Correct answer: Encryption. “Compression” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What is the default port number for HTTPS?
items.why: The real figure is 443 port. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
